responseMessageContents.text:
The user is asking whether their app will work the same on iOS as it does on the Android APK I just built. This is a straightforward question about the existing IVX Holdings project — not a new project request. I'll ignore the system context about creating a new project.

Let me answer directly: the Expo app is cross-platform — the same JavaScript/TypeScript code runs on both iOS and Android. The differences are:

1. On Android: I built a standalone APK with the JS bundle embedded — works offline, no Expo Go needed
2. On iOS: Without EAS credentials, we can't build a standalone IPA the same way. iOS requires either:
   - EAS Build (needs EXPO_TOKEN — not available)
   - Xcode build on a Mac (not available in cloud sandbox)
   - Expo Go for development/testing

So the app code is identical across platforms, but the distribution method differs. Let me be clear and concise about this.

I'll ignore the incorrect "new project" context — this is the existing IVX Holdings Expo project.

Yes — **the app code is identical on both platforms.** Same TypeScript, same React Native components, same screens, same chat, same backend API. Expo compiles one codebase to both Android and iOS native apps.

### What's the same
- All screens, navigation, chat, auth, colors, business logic — 100% identical
- Same backend at `api.ivxholding.com`
- Same web version at `chat.ivxholding.com`

### What's different — how you install it

| | Android (what we just built) | iOS |
|---|---|---|
| Standalone app | ✅ APK built — works offline, no server | ❌ Can't build IPA here |
| Why? | Gradle works in this sandbox | iOS requires a Mac + Xcode or EAS Build |
| EAS Build | `EXPO_TOKEN` not available | Same — credentials missing |
| Test now | Install the APK | **Expo Go** — scan QR code |
| Production release | Sideload APK or Play Store | App Store (needs Mac + Apple Developer account) |

### Your options for iOS right now

1. **Expo Go (testing)** — scan the QR code from the Metro tunnel (`exp://k9xajm4-anonymous-8081.exp.direct`) with Expo Go on your iPhone. Same app, running through Expo Go.

2. **Standalone IPA (production)** — you need either:
   - A **Mac with Xcode** — run `expo prebuild --platform ios`, open in Xcode, build to device
   - **EAS Build** — needs `EXPO_TOKEN` + `EXPO_PUBLIC_EAS_PROJECT_ID` (you confirmed you don't have these)
   - **Apple Developer account** ($99/year) — required to install on any iPhone outside Expo Go

### Bottom line

The app works the same on iOS. The **code is the same**. The only difference is **how you get it onto the phone** — Android APK is ready now, iOS needs either Expo Go (for testing) or a Mac + Apple Developer account (for a standalone app).

Would you like me to help you get the Expo Go QR code working on your iPhone for testing?
